nouns.meaning: A small furry animal that says meow
nouns.example: My cat sleeps on my bed and purrs when I pet her.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A cat is a pet that says 'meow'.

nouns.meaning: A friendly animal that says woof and loves to play
nouns.example: My dog wags his tail when I come home from school.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A dog is a pet that barks and likes to play fetch.

nouns.meaning: An animal with feathers and wings that can fly
nouns.example: The red bird sits on the tree branch and sings a pretty song.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A bird is an animal that has feathers and can fly.

nouns.meaning: An animal that lives in water and has fins
nouns.example: The orange fish swims around the castle in its tank.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A fish is an animal that lives in water.

nouns.meaning: A building where people live
nouns.example: My house has a red door and a big backyard for playing.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A house is where you and your family live.

nouns.meaning: A vehicle with four wheels that drives on roads
nouns.example: My dad drives the blue car to work every morning.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A car is a machine that takes you places.

nouns.meaning: A round object used in games and sports
nouns.example: I kick the red ball into the goal and score a point!
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A ball is a round toy you can throw or kick.

nouns.meaning: Pages with words and pictures stuck together
nouns.example: My favorite book has a dragon, a princess, and a knight.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A book has stories and pictures inside.

nouns.meaning: Something fun to play with
nouns.example: My new toy is a robot that lights up and makes sounds.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A toy is something you play with for fun.

nouns.meaning: Someone you like to spend time with
nouns.example: My best friend sits next to me at lunch every day.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A friend is someone you like to play with.

nouns.meaning: The people you live with or are related to
nouns.example: My family has mom, dad, my sister, and me.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: Your family is the people who love and take care of you.

nouns.meaning: Your female parent
nouns.example: My mom gives me a hug and a kiss every morning.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: Mom is the lady who is your parent.

nouns.meaning: Your male parent
nouns.example: My dad makes pancakes on Sunday mornings.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: Dad is the man who is your parent.

nouns.meaning: A person who helps you learn at school
nouns.example: My teacher reads us stories and teaches us math.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A teacher helps you learn new things.

nouns.meaning: A place where children go to learn
nouns.example: I go to school from Monday to Friday to learn and play.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: School is where you learn reading, math, and science.

nouns.meaning: The day you were born, celebrated each year
nouns.example: On my birthday, I eat cake and open presents.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: Your birthday is the special day you were born.

nouns.meaning: A gift given to someone
nouns.example: I got a new puzzle as a present for my birthday.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: A present is something nice someone gives you.

nouns.meaning: A sweet baked food often eaten at parties
nouns.example: The chocolate cake has sprinkles and a candle on top.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: Cake is a sweet dessert with frosting.

nouns.meaning: A clear liquid that people and animals drink
nouns.example: I drink water when I am thirsty because it is healthy.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: Water is what you drink from the faucet or a bottle.

nouns.meaning: The big bright star that gives us light and warmth
nouns.example: The sun feels warm on my face when I play outside.
nouns.kid_friendly_explanation: The sun is the big yellow light in the sky.
